title: Week 03 - Counting and Classification
description: Practice conditional counting, classification statistics, and using multiple counters.

questions.title: Count How Many Even Numbers There Are from 1 to n

description.background: The teacher gives a positive integer n and asks students to count from 1 to n, then count how many of the numbers are even.
description.task: Count the even numbers from 1 to n and return the result.
description.output: Return the number of even numbers from 1 to n.
description.hint: Loop through the numbers and use a count variable to record how many meet the condition.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n)
    local count = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        if i % 2 == 0 then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end

    return count
end

questions.title: Count How Many Multiples of 5 There Are from 1 to n

description.background: In a school activity, a special task is arranged every 5 numbers. Now the teacher wants to know how many numbers from 1 to n are multiples of 5.
description.task: Count how many numbers from 1 to n are divisible by 5.
description.output: Return the number of multiples of 5.
description.hint: Check whether each number can be divided evenly by the target number.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n)
    local count = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        if i % 5 == 0 then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end

    return count
end

questions.title: Count How Many Odd Task Numbers There Are

description.background: Game tasks are numbered from 1 to n. Odd-numbered tasks are normal tasks, and even-numbered tasks are special tasks. The system needs to count the normal tasks.
description.task: Count how many odd numbers there are from 1 to n.
description.output: Return the number of odd task numbers.
description.hint: You can use the remainder after division by 2 to decide the number type.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n)
    local count = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        if i % 2 ~= 0 then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end

    return count
end

questions.title: Count Numbers That Are Even and Greater Than the Target

description.background: The teacher wants to select some students from the numbers 1 to n. The rule is that a number must be even and must be greater than target.
description.task: Count how many numbers from 1 to n satisfy both conditions.
description.output: Return the number of values that satisfy the conditions.
description.hint: First decide which conditions a number must satisfy, then decide whether to count it.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n, target)
    local count = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        if i % 2 == 0 and i > target then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end

    return count
end

questions.title: Count Game Levels That Can Receive Reward Coins

description.background: A game has n levels. The system gives rewards starting from levels that are multiples of rewardStep, but a reward level is valid only when its level number is at least minLevel.
description.task: Count how many levels from 1 to n can receive reward coins.
description.input: Three numbers: n, rewardStep, and minLevel.
description.output: Return the number of levels that can receive rewards.
description.hint: A level must satisfy both the number rule and the minimum-level requirement.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n, rewardStep, minLevel)
    local count = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        if i % rewardStep == 0 and i >= minLevel then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end

    return count
end

questions.title: Count Numbers Divisible by 3 but Not Even

description.background: Student numbers run from 1 to n. The teacher wants to find a special kind of number: it is divisible by 3, but it must not be even.
description.task: Count how many numbers from 1 to n satisfy the rule.
description.output: Return the number of values that satisfy the condition.
description.hint: You can put the multiple check and the odd-even check in the same test.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n)
    local count = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        if i % 3 == 0 and i % 2 ~= 0 then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end

    return count
end

questions.title: Split Student Numbers into Three Remainder Groups and Count Them

description.background: The teacher wants to split students numbered from 1 to n into three groups. Each student enters a group based on the remainder after dividing the number by 3.
description.task: Count the numbers with remainder 0, remainder 1, and remainder 2.
description.output: Return the counts for remainders 0, 1, and 2 in order.
description.hint: Prepare multiple count variables, with each variable responsible for one category.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n)
    local count0 = 0
    local count1 = 0
    local count2 = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        if i % 3 == 0 then
            count0 = count0 + 1
        elseif i % 3 == 1 then
            count1 = count1 + 1
        else
            count2 = count2 + 1
        end
    end

    return count0, count1, count2
end

questions.title: Count Normal, Hard, and Hidden Game Tasks by Number Rules

description.background: Game tasks are numbered from 1 to n. A task is hidden if its number is a multiple of 10; if it is not hidden but is divisible by 3, it is hard; all other tasks are normal.
description.task: Count the numbers of normal tasks, hard tasks, and hidden tasks.
description.output: Return the normal count, hard count, and hidden count in order.
description.hint: Pay attention to the order of the classification rules.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n)
    local normal = 0
    local hard = 0
    local hidden = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        if i % 10 == 0 then
            hidden = hidden + 1
        elseif i % 3 == 0 then
            hard = hard + 1
        else
            normal = normal + 1
        end
    end

    return normal, hard, hidden
end

questions.title: Compare How Many Numbers Two Reward Rules Select

description.background: A game system has two reward rules. The first rule rewards numbers that are multiples of a, and the second rule rewards numbers that are multiples of b. The designer wants to know which rule selects more numbers.
description.task: Count how many numbers from 1 to n match each rule, then return the rule with the larger count.
description.input: Three numbers: n, a, and b.
description.output: Return "first" if the first rule has more matches, "second" if the second rule has more matches, and "same" if they have the same count.
description.hint: Count the two rules separately first, then compare the results.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n, a, b)
    local countA = 0
    local countB = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        if i % a == 0 then
            countA = countA + 1
        end

        if i % b == 0 then
            countB = countB + 1
        end
    end

    if countA > countB then
        return "first"
    elseif countB > countA then
        return "second"
    end

    return "same"
end
